message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Bar Cecil (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 547 reviews; Google review count [UNVERIFIED] but Yelp + Tripadvisor + OpenTable + press footprint indicates a mid-to-large review band (estimated 1000-2000+ across platforms)
- Sentiment: mostly positive — 4.5 stars on Yelp; consistent praise for design, service, food (deviled eggs, pork chop, date-and-sage butter bread rolls), and bartender expertise; minor friction is reservation difficulty rather than the experience itself
- Longevity: Opened 2021 (approximately 5 years operating as of 2026); ownership has been stable — lead partner John Janulis with majority owners Richard Crisman and Jeff Brock of Sparrows Lodge / Holiday House; adjacent lounge Beaton's added as expansion
- Credentials: ["Michelin Guide recommended (inaugural California-Palm Springs coverage; Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025)","The Infatuation: named favorite date-night spot in Palm Springs; included in 25 Best Restaurants & Bars in Palm Springs","Time Out feature framing it as 'the hardest reservation in Palm Springs' (May 2025)","OpenTable Diners' Choice (2024)","Palm Springs Life feature ('A Neighborhood Place')","WWD feature ('Dandy in the Desert')","InsideHook feature ('How Bar Cecil Became the Hottest Reservation in Palm Springs')"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'best restaurants Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs', 'hardest reservation Palm Springs', 'Michelin Palm Springs', 'best cocktail bar Palm Springs'. Surfaces moderately for: 'best restaurants Coachella Valley'. Does NOT cleanly surface for: 'French restaurant Palm Springs / Coachella Valley' — agents return Chez Pierre, Le Vallauris, French Rotisserie Cafe instead, because Bar Cecil is categorized as American/cocktail bar despite French-leaning menu. Does not surface for Palm Desert, Rancho Mirage, La Quinta, or Indio queries (correctly — it's PS-specific).
- Site structured data: Weak. barcecil.com is built on Showit. No JSON-LD schema.org Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup detected. No visible OpenGraph metadata extracted. Hours, address, and phone are present as plain text only. Menus are served as static PDFs (Showit-hosted), which agents cannot parse as structured menu data. Site relies on OpenTable embed and Google Maps link for downstream structured signals.
- Visibility gap score: 3
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt: Look at the raw HTML source. Report: 1) Is there any JSON-LD schema.org markup (Restaurant, LocalBusiness)? 2) Is there OpenGraph metadata? 3) Is the site built on Showit? 4) How are address, hours, phone presented (text vs structured)? 5) Are menus PDFs or HTML?

content.content:
# Bar Cecil Website Analysis

**1) JSON-LD Schema.org Markup:**
No Restaurant or LocalBusiness schema.org markup is present in the provided HTML.

**2) OpenGraph Metadata:**
No OpenGraph metadata tags are included in this content.

**3) Showit Platform:**
Yes, the site is built on Showit. Multiple PDF menu links reference `static.showit.co` domain paths containing restaurant documents.

**4) Address, Hours, Phone Presentation:**
All contact information is presented as plain text only—no structured data markup:
- Address: "1555 S Palm Canyon Dr. Suite H-104 Palm Springs CA 92264"
- Hours: "Open Daily: 5 pm – 10 pm" with "Kitchen closes at 9pm"
- Phone: "442-332-3800"

**5) Menu Format:**
All menus are PDFs hosted on Showit's CDN, including Cocktails, Wine, Food, and Spirits menus. No HTML-based menu pages exist in this content.

Based on the search results, here's what I found:

Bar Cecil has been one of the most popular reservations in Palm Springs since opening in 2021, if not the most difficult one in the greater Coachella Valley. 

The intimate 75-seat restaurant's name and colorful, maximalist interior design are inspired by Sir Cecil Beaton, a late British photographer, designer and queer icon. Chef partner Gabriel Woo, a Palm Springs native, incorporates produce from San Diego's Girl & Dug Farm and locally grown dates into the seasonally inspired, French-leaning menu.

Online reservations open at exactly 8 am PST 2 weeks in advance, making them notoriously difficult to secure. The Time Out review praised the restaurant's offerings, including the steak frites and pork chop, and Bar Cecil earned five stars from Time Out.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bar Cecil Palm Springs and its Michelin Guide recognition:

## Michelin Guide Recognition

Bar Cecil has earned consecutive Michelin Plates in 2024 and 2025. The Michelin Plate designation is awarded to restaurants that inspectors identify as serving good food, one tier below Bib Gourmand and star level.

## Restaurant Details

Bar Cecil is located at 1555 S Palm Canyon Drive, Unit H-104, in the southern section of the Palm Canyon corridor. The restaurant is framed as a tribute to Cecil Beaton's particular strain of classical rebellion, with the room trading in atmosphere as much as food.

The Michelin Guide calls Bar Cecil's ambiance both "garish and chic." The restaurant features sumptuous design with walls splashed with an impressive private collection of modern art (including pieces by Calder and Warhol).

## Cuisine and Menu

The restaurant offers an American dining program with European and bistro influences. It's described as a buzzing, art-filled brasserie where reservations disappear fast, serving upscale comfort food with a stylish twist, featuring items like oysters on the half shell, steak tartare, roasted king crab legs, and a signature burger elevated with melted Comté and house sauce.

**Bar Cecil Owners:**
Bar Cecil is owned by Richard Crisman and Jeff Brock, who are life partners and also work on other desert hospitality projects together like Sparrows Lodge and Holiday House. Bar Cecil was created in partnership with John Janulis, whose Lightning Bar Collective is behind some of Portland's most popular bars, and the Villa Royale hotel in Palm Springs.

**Background on the Owners:**
Richard Crisman is a former executive vice president of marketing for Old Navy. Jeff Brock and Richard Crisman are entrepreneurs who own two hugely popular Palm Springs boutique hotels, Sparrows Lodge and Holiday House.

**About the Restaurant:**
Inspired by the aesthetic of rebel photographer and costume designer Cecil Beaton, the new restaurant quickly became a Palm Springs essential. The venue earned a coveted spot in the Michelin Guide.

Based on my search results, here are some of the best restaurants for date night in Palm Springs for 2026:

## Top Romantic Restaurants:

**Copley's on Palm Canyon** - Set in the former Cary Grant estate, Copley's exudes old Hollywood glamour with contemporary American cuisine. The restaurant features multiple dining areas including intimate indoor rooms and a stunning outdoor patio surrounded by lush gardens and mountain views, with elevated dishes using locally sourced ingredients.

**4 Saints** - Rooftop dining atop the Kimpton Rowan Hotel provides sweeping views of the surrounding San Jacinto mountains. Elegant, modern American cuisine is served with touches from the surrounding region, like local oranges, avocados, dates, and tomatoes infused into sauces throughout the menu.

**Il Corso** - Il Corso offers an authentic Italian dining experience, with reviews praising its 'beautiful ambiance' and 'excellent food and service', with lovely atmosphere and impeccable service making it perfect for a date night.

**FARM** - FARM is a traditional Provencial-style restaurant in downtown Palm Springs with a menu comprised of traditional European dishes using locally sourced produce and artisanal meats, served at a pace so you can enjoy your evening with outdoor seating that transports you to the South of France.

**Birba** - A favorite among locals, Birba combines a hip outdoor setting with a menu inspired by Italian street food, featuring a beautiful tree-filled patio with fire pits and a wood-fired oven producing exceptional pizzas.

**Lavender Bistro** - Lavender combines the finest meats and seafood with the freshest organic ingredients, with indoor dining in a warm, cozy atmosphere or on a stunning tree-lined patio, while providing superb live music nightly.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best French restaurants in Palm Springs:

## Top Recommendations

**Le Vallauris** - For over 30 years, Le Vallauris has been one of Palm Springs' most iconic fine dining destinations. Guests rave about the classic dishes, from Dover sole to filet mignon, and the romantic garden patio, shaded by ficus trees and surrounded by fresh flowers.

**L'Atelier Café** - Located in La Plaza, L'Atelier Café brings an authentic slice of Paris to downtown Palm Springs with owners straight from the City of Lights. The quiches alone are worth a visit, but the sandwiches steal hearts: French ham flown in from Paris, prosciutto with creamy mozzarella, croque monsieur, and croque madame on impossibly crisp baguettes.

**Pomme Frite** - For a more casual yet equally delightful French experience, Pomme Frite is a must-visit, located at 155 S Palm Canyon Dr, this cozy bistro brings the flavors of Belgium and France to the desert. Pomme Frite offers an array of French classics, including moules frites (mussels with fries), croque monsieur, and a variety of savory crepes, and is a favorite among locals and tourists alike who crave authentic French comfort food.

**Freddie's Kitchen** - A more recent addition to the Palm Springs culinary scene, Freddie's Kitchen at The Cole offers a contemporary spin on French cuisine, serving French-inspired dishes with a modern twist.

Based on the search results, here's what The Infatuation says about Bar Cecil in their Palm Springs review:

Reservations at this American bar/restaurant get nabbed weeks in advance, but the bar area is walk-in only and that's where you'll want to sit anyway. The entire menu is offered at the bar (get the pork chop), plus you can chat up the bartenders about the art on the walls while they pour you an Old Fashioned. 

Bar Cecil is one of those places that's sexy and chic without feeling like "sexy and chic" is the theme. It just naturally is—while also being their favorite place to grab dinner in town. The Infatuation gave the restaurant an 8 rating, which means it's "great" and "worth making an effort or crossing town for."

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bar Cecil in Palm Springs:

## Opening & History

Bar Cecil opened in April 2021 at 1555 S Palm Canyon Dr in the Plaza del Sol Shopping Center, in a space that was formerly occupied by Kiyosaku Japanese restaurant. Since opening in 2021, Bar Cecil has become one of the most popular reservations in Palm Springs, if not the most difficult one in the greater Coachella Valley.

## Design & Inspiration

The restaurant's name and colorful, maximalist interior design are inspired by Sir Cecil Beaton, the late British photographer, designer. The design features walls splashed with an impressive private collection of modern art including pieces by Calder and Warhol.

## Ownership & Leadership

The restaurant is the brainchild of a group of successful Palm Springs hotelier-restaurateurs with ties to properties like Holiday House, Sparrows Lodge and the Pink Cabana. The culinary team is led by partner-Executive Chef Gabriel Woo, former executive chef of Sparrow's Lodge and Holiday House in Palm Springs.

## Dining Experience

The intimate 75-seat restaurant is located in the Plaza Del Sol Shopping Center, and reservations book weeks in advance.

content.text: I have enough verification. Let me finalize.

confirms: Yelp review count: 547 reviews verified directly via Yelp listing title (June 2026)
Opened 2021 (April 2021) — approximately 5 years operating as of 2026
Ownership stable — Richard Crisman and Jeff Brock (Sparrows Lodge / Holiday House) with John Janulis (Lightning Bar Collective / Villa Royale) as partner; Gabriel Woo as chef-partner
Beaton's lounge expansion confirmed (beatonsatbarcecil.com and separate Yelp listing for 'Beaton's at Bar Cecil')
Michelin Guide listing confirmed (guide.michelin.com page for Bar Cecil); Michelin Plate-tier inclusion in California/Palm Springs coverage
Time Out 'hardest reservation in Palm Springs' framing (May 2025 review) confirmed verbatim
Infatuation review exists for Bar Cecil and rates it 8/10 ('great, worth crossing town for')
Does NOT cleanly surface for 'French restaurant Palm Springs' queries — Le Vallauris, Chez Pierre, L'Atelier, Pomme Frite, French Rotisserie Café surface instead
Site structured data weak — barcecil.com confirmed on Showit, no JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup, no OpenGraph, address/hours/phone as plain text, menus as Showit-hosted PDFs
OpenTable presence strong (4.8 stars across 1,858 OpenTable reviews per current snapshot)
contradicts: Claim 'Surfaces strongly for date night Palm Springs' is overstated — independent date-night queries for Palm Springs surfaced Copley's, 4 Saints, Il Corso, FARM, Birba, Lavender Bistro in top results; Bar Cecil did not appear in the AI synthesis of top date-night picks despite Infatuation calling it a favorite date-night spot. Visibility for this query is moderate, not strong.

Cuisine framing: Bar Cecil's menu is widely described as 'French-leaning' / European bistro influences (Time Out, Desert Diner, Michelin all note this), yet the claim concedes it's categorized as American/cocktail bar. This is accurate as a gap, but the assessment underplays that multiple authoritative sources (Time Out: 'French-leaning menu'; Michelin: 'European and bistro influences') explicitly use French framing — agents could be coaxed to surface it for French queries with better structured data, so the gap is somewhat self-inflicted via missing schema.
flagged_as_unverified: Specific 2024 OpenTable Diners' Choice award designation — could not independently confirm the specific 2024 badge in search results (only general high OpenTable rating verified)
Palm Springs Life 'A Neighborhood Place' feature title — URL surfaced in results but full article verification not completed
WWD 'Dandy in the Desert' headline — URL surfaced (wwd.com 1234800825) but not directly fetched
InsideHook 'How Bar Cecil Became the Hottest Reservation in Palm Springs' — URL surfaced but not fetched
Infatuation specifically naming it 'favorite date-night spot in Palm Springs' — review exists and is positive, but the exact 'favorite date-night spot' phrasing not directly verified in snippets
Inaugural Michelin California-Palm Springs coverage timing — Michelin listing confirmed but the 'inaugural' framing not independently dated
Cross-platform total review band of 1000-2000+ — OpenTable alone shows 1,858 reviews, so the band is plausible/likely on the higher end, but full cross-platform tally not summed

Sentiment specifics (deviled eggs, pork chop, date-and-sage butter bread rolls praise) — pork chop confirmed via Infatuation/Time Out; deviled eggs and date-sage rolls not specifically surfaced in this verification pass
input.notes: Core agent-visibility assessment holds up well. The big load-bearing claims — Yelp 547/4.5, 2021 opening, Crisman/Brock/Janulis ownership, Michelin recognition, Time Out 'hardest reservation' headline, Showit + no JSON-LD, French-restaurant query gap — are all independently confirmed. The 'date night Palm Springs' strength claim is the main soft spot: Bar Cecil did not surface in top date-night results from an independent query, suggesting moderate (not strong) surfacing despite Infatuation's framing. Visibility gap score of 3 and convertible tier are reasonable: no JSON-LD, no OG, PDF menus, and a French-menu/American-category mismatch are all real, fixable gaps. A few press-feature exact-headline claims remain unverified at the headline level but URLs exist in SERPs, so they're plausible.
